task_completion.description: Check the status of Docker containers
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window displaying the output of a command like `docker ps`, showing the Jitsi containers (web, prosody, jicofo, jvb) and their statuses.
Terminal window displaying the output of a command like `docker network inspect meet.jitsi` or `docker network ls`, showing network details such as the subnet.

task_completion.description: Verify the accessibility of the Web UI
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to `http://localhost:8080`, displaying the Jitsi Meet interface.

task_completion.description: Verify meeting functionality in the test room
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser specifically open to the URL `http://localhost:8080/health-check-test`, showing the active Jitsi meeting interface (e.g., meeting controls, join screen).

task_completion.description: Produce the final audit report with required sections
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, gedit) or terminal output showing the contents of `~/jitsi_audit_report.txt` with the required sections: CONTAINER STATUS, SERVICE CONNECTIVITY, NETWORK CONFIGURATION, MEETING FUNCTIONALITY, and SUMMARY.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to test the Web UI and meeting functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an actual web browser (like Chromium or Firefox) being used to navigate to the Jitsi URLs, rather than just terminal commands like curl.

integrity.description: Agent executed actual Docker commands to gather system information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show terminal commands like `docker ps` and `docker network inspect` being run and returning real system output.

integrity.description: The audit report contains genuine data gathered from the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The information in the report (e.g., container uptimes, subnet, network name) matches the output seen in the terminal and browser, rather than being hallucinated or copied from an example.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is /home/ga/jitsi_audit_report.txt. The report must contain exactly these sections: CONTAINER STATUS, SERVICE CONNECTIVITY, NETWORK CONFIGURATION, MEETING FUNCTIONALITY, and SUMMARY. The Jitsi deployment uses stable-9753 images (jitsi/web, jitsi/prosody, jitsi/jicofo, jitsi/jvb) connected via a bridge network named 'meet.jitsi'. The Web UI is at http://localhost:8080.
